danger_signals.visual_form: Character animation intensity and speed increases during combat
danger_signals.meaning: Active threat - opponent is actively attacking

danger_signals.visual_form: Health bar visually decreasing on HUD
danger_signals.meaning: Player danger - character close to defeat or submission

danger_signals.visual_form: Jumbo Flapjack and Scorpion have larger sprite sizes and distinctive visual designs
danger_signals.meaning: Escalated threat level - non-playable boss wrestlers are mechanically dangerous

danger_signals.visual_form: Character poses and animation sequences before executing finisher moves

safety_signals.visual_form: Character neutral stance, breathing animations, no active combat
safety_signals.meaning: Temporary reprieve - no active threat during idle animations

safety_signals.visual_form: Opponent on ground with referee count animation, visual indicator of match control
Belt displayed when defeating roster, visual progression marker

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Character design themes reflect national origins (Russian, Mexican luchador, Japanese kabuki, English, Australian)
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: No faction-specific enemy variants documented; characters maintain consistent visual identity
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Ring environment (appears arena-based); character entrance style may reflect cultural theme

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Mike Haggar visual continuity from Final Fight series; Jessica (his daughter) appears as celebration character
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Haggar is narrative anchor - appears as returning character with established Metropolitan authority

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Biff Slamkovich resembles Street Fighter III's Alex; Gunloc implied as Guile's brother
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Cross-universe character design consistency suggests shared Capcom fighting game universe

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: One-on-one ring configuration; championship belt stakes displayed
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Series of sequential matches against CPU roster; player must defeat all opponents to win championship, then defend against entire roster

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Two-on-two ring setup; boss characters Jumbo and Scorpion become selectable
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Tag team mechanics enabled; cooperative two-player gameplay versus paired opponents

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Belt holder status displayed; opponents enter with heightened visual intensity
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Player must defend belt against entire roster in succession; mechanical difficulty escalation

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Title screen differs between Muscle Bomber (JP) and Saturday Night Slam Masters (US/EU); Victor Ortega presence/removal on title screen
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Mechanical gameplay identical, but narrative framing and character names localized

environmental_storytelling.example: 10 wrestler roster representing distinct national/cultural identities (Russian Biff, Mexican El Stingray, Japanese Great Oni, English Titanic Tim, Australian Alexander, Jamaican King Rasta Mon) communicate world-state as international wrestling competition
Tetsuo Hara's promotional art (Fist of the North Star artist) applied to character designs and SNES ports' redrawn attract sequences; visual aesthetic communicates manga/anime action narrative
King Rasta Mon accompanied by pet monkey Freak (manager); Jessica appears to celebrate Haggar victories - companion presence communicates character's social/professional context
Jumbo Flapjack and Scorpion visually larger and only accessible as non-playable bosses in Single Match, then selectable in Team mode - scale and availability communicate progression hierarchy

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Trained wrestler with Haggar connection (JP version); makes 'Comrade Zangief' reference in losing quotes - demonstrates cross-universe narrative awareness
Rivals with Biff (JP version); implied Guile relative (US version) - visual animation style suggests trained fighting technique
Kabuki-themed wrestler with ritualistic posturing; rivalry with El Stingray suggests competing fighting philosophies
High-flying luchador technique; speed-based movement animation versus size-based wrestlers - communicates technique-based fighting strategy
Oversized sprite representation; size-and-strength fighting style; ex-tag partner to Street Fighter's Birdie - scale conveys intimidation tactic
Savage beast-like animations and behavior; monkey manager Freak present ringside - visual performance communicates unpredictable, primal fighting approach
Very large sadistic wrestler; non-playable until Team Battle mode; largest sprite representation - size and boss status communicate peak difficulty threat
Non-playable boss in Single Match; selectable in Team Battle - thematic name (Scorpion) suggests aggressive/venomous fighting style

notes: Saturday Night Slam Masters communicates world-state primarily through character visual design, national/cultural identity markers, and roster hierarchy (playable vs. boss). The game lacks explicit political faction systems but uses international wrestling competition framework to establish geopolitical diversity. Danger signals are minimal and action-focused rather than narrative-driven. Visual storytelling relies heavily on character design aesthetic (Tetsuo Hara's manga-influenced art style), cultural costume/theme consistency, and sprite-based scale differentiation. The game's Capcom universe integration (Haggar from Final Fight, Gunloc/Guile connection, Zangief reference) creates implicit narrative world-state without explicit visual markers. Regional differences (Muscle Bomber vs. Saturday Night Slam Masters) are primarily title screen and character name modifications, not mechanically significant world-state changes.
sources_used: tcrf - Saturday Night Slam Masters (SNES) technical documentation
wikipedia - Saturday Night Slam Masters gameplay and character roster information
